html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 伏牺龙身 - Refers to Fuxi, a legendary figure in Chinese mythology, often depicted with a dragon body. This phrase emphasizes his divine nature.
  • 女娲蛇躯 - Refers to Nüwa, another legendary figure, often depicted with a snake body. This phrase similarly emphasizes her divine nature.
  • 颛顼氏三子俱亡 - Refers to the three sons of the legendary ruler Zhuanxu who all died prematurely. This phrase introduces a mythological narrative.
  • 处人宫室 - Means 'dwelling in human palaces,' suggesting a supernatural presence in human dwellings.
  • 善惊小儿 - Means 'good at frightening children,' indicating a malevolent aspect of the sons.
  • 五营千骑 - Refers to a military unit consisting of five camps and a thousand cavalrymen, used for ceremonial purposes.
  • 自端门传炬送疫 - Means 'from the main gate, carrying torches to send off the plague,' describing a ritual to expel illness.
  • 弃洛水中 - Means 'thrown into the Luo River,' indicating the disposal of the plague.
  • 荆楚岁时记 - Refers to the 'Records of Customs and Festivals of Jingchu,' a historical text documenting local customs and festivals.
  • 玉烛宝典 - Refers to the 'Jade Candle Treasury,' a historical text containing various rituals and customs.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 伏牺 - Fuxi, a legendary figure in Chinese mythology, considered one of the Three Sovereigns.
  • 女娲 - Nüwa, a legendary figure in Chinese mythology, often associated with creation and repair.
  • 颛顼 - Zhuanxu, a legendary ruler in Chinese mythology, known for his wisdom and governance.
  • 五营千骑 - A military unit in ancient China, used for ceremonial and protective purposes.
  • 端门 - The main gate of the imperial palace, symbolizing the center of power.
  • 洛水 - The Luo River, a significant river in Chinese mythology and history.
  • 荆楚 - Refers to the region of Jingchu, an area in central China.
  • 玉烛宝典 - A historical text that documents various rituals and customs, including those related to health and disease.

Proper Nouns

  • 伏牺 - Fuxi
  • 女娲 - Nüwa
  • 颛顼 - Zhuanxu
  • 五营千骑 - Five camps and a thousand cavalrymen
  • 端门 - Main gate of the imperial palace
  • 洛水 - Luo River
  • 荆楚岁时记 - Records of Customs and Festivals of Jingchu
  • 玉烛宝典 - Jade Candle Treasury

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 與帝爭神 - Contend with the emperor as a deity, likely referring to a mythological conflict between gods or supernatural beings.
  • 斷其首 - Cut off his head, a violent act often associated with mythological or heroic narratives.
  • 葬之常羊山 - Buried him on Chángyáng Mountain, indicating the location of a burial site.
  • 以乳為目，以齊為口 - Metaphorical expressions describing a transformation or mutation, where '乳' (breast) becomes '目' (eye) and '齊' (a place name, Qí) becomes '口' (mouth).
  • 御覽五百五十引山海經注云玄中記亦載 - Refers to a citation from the *Shanhaijing* (Classic of Mountains and Seas) and *Xuanzhongji* (Records of the Mysterious Middle), indicating that these texts also contain similar stories.
  • 尹壽作鏡 - Yin Shou made a mirror, but the context of the mirror's creation or significance is unclear.
  • 旬始作冠 - Xun Shi made a hat, but the significance of the hat or the figure Xun Shi is unclear.
  • 狗封氏者 - Title or designation for a group of people, likely referring to a tribe or lineage.
  • 高辛氏有美女 - Gaoxin had a beautiful woman, referring to a figure from mythology or legend.
  • 犬戎為亂 - The Xianrong caused chaos, referring to a group of nomadic tribes that were often at odds with Chinese states.
  • 帝曰，有討之者，妻以美女，封三百戶 - The emperor said, 'whoever subdues them, I will marry his daughter to him and grant him 300 households,' indicating a reward for military service.
  • 帝之狗名槃護 - The emperor's dog was named Panhu, referring to a legendary figure.
  • 三月而殺犬戎 - In three months, he killed the Xianrong, indicating a swift military victory.
  • 以其首來 - He brought back their heads, a common practice in ancient warfare.
  • 帝以為不可訓民 - The emperor thought it was not suitable to train the people, suggesting that the method of using a dog to subdue the Xianrong was not appropriate for human governance.
  • 乃妻以女流之 - He then married his daughter to him and sent him to live in exile, indicating a change in the emperor's decision.
  • 會稽東南二萬一千里，得海中土 - In the southeast of Kuaiji, 21,000 li, he obtained land in the sea, indicating a distant location.
  • 方三千里 - Covering an area of 3,000 li, indicating the size of the territory.
  • 生男為狗，生女為美女 - Those born as males became dogs, and those born as females became beautiful women, indicating a transformation or curse.
  • 封為狗民国 - He was enfeoffed as the ruler of a dog kingdom, indicating a unique political status.
  • 丈夫民 - Title or designation for a group of people, likely referring to a tribe or lineage.
  • 殷帝太戊 - Emperor Taipu of Yin, referring to a historical figure.
  • 使王英采藥於西王母 - He sent Wang Ying to gather herbs from the Queen Mother of the West, referring to a mythological figure.
  • 至此絕糧，不能進 - Upon reaching this point, they ran out of food and could not proceed, indicating a difficult journey.
  • 乃食木實，衣以木皮 - They then ate the fruit of trees and wore tree bark, indicating survival in the wilderness.
  • 終身無妻，產子二人，從背?間出 - He remained unmarried for life, and two children were born from his back, indicating a miraculous birth.
  • 是為丈夫民 - This is the origin of the husbandmen, indicating the origin of a group of people.
  • 去玉門二萬里 - 20,000 li from Yumen, indicating a distant location.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 刑天 - A mythological figure known for his battle with the Jade Emperor (Jade Emperor).
  • 帝 - Refers to the Jade Emperor or a high-ranking deity.
  • 常羊山 - A mountain in mythology, often associated with the burial of mythological figures.
  • 玄中記 - A book of myths and legends, often cited in classical texts.
  • 尹壽 - A historical or mythological figure known for making mirrors.
  • 旬始 - A historical or mythological figure known for making hats.
  • 狗封氏 - A tribe or lineage known for their unique customs and transformations.
  • 高辛氏 - A legendary figure, often associated with the Yellow Emperor's lineage.
  • 犬戎 - A nomadic tribe often at odds with Chinese states.
  • 槃護 - A legendary dog, often associated with the myth of the dog people.
  • 西王母 - A mythological figure, often associated with the Queen Mother of the West.
  • 王英 - A historical or mythological figure known for gathering herbs.
  • 玉門 - A city or region, often associated with the western frontier of the Chinese empire.

Proper Nouns

  • 刑天 - A mythological figure.
  • 帝 - The Jade Emperor or a high-ranking deity.
  • 常羊山 - A mountain in mythology.
  • 玄中記 - A book of myths and legends.
  • 尹壽 - A historical or mythological figure.
  • 旬始 - A historical or mythological figure.
  • 狗封氏 - A tribe or lineage.
  • 高辛氏 - A legendary figure.
  • 犬戎 - A nomadic tribe.
  • 槃護 - A legendary dog.
  • 西王母 - A mythological figure.
  • 王英 - A historical or mythological figure.
  • 玉門 - A city or region.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 扶伏民 - A term referring to a specific group of people, likely a reference to a mythical or historical tribe. The exact meaning is unclear, but it seems to describe a people who lived in a particular region.
  • 刑而放之 - Literally means 'punished and then exiled,' indicating a form of punishment involving banishment.
  • 扶伏而去 - Means 'to crawl away,' suggesting a humble or submissive manner of departure.
  • 化民 - Refers to a group of people who undergo transformation or change, possibly due to environmental or cultural factors.
  • 食叶三七年化 - Literally means 'eat leaves for three to seven years and transform,' indicating a process of metamorphosis.
  • 能以自裹如蚕绩 - Means 'can wrap themselves like silkworms spinning silk,' describing a unique behavior.
  • 九年生翼 - Means 'nine years to grow wings,' indicating a significant developmental stage.
  • 十年而死 - Means 'die after ten years,' suggesting a lifespan of ten years.
  • 奇肱氏 - Refers to a mythical figure known for his skill in crafting flying vehicles.
  • 善奇巧 - Means 'skilled in strange and ingenious crafts,' highlighting the figure's exceptional abilities.
  • 君子之国 - Refers to a land of the virtuous or noble people.
  • 地方千里 - Means 'an area of a thousand li,' indicating a large territory.
  • 多木槿之华 - Means 'abundant with hibiscus flowers,' describing the natural scenery.
  • 伊俗与唐吾同俗 - Means 'the customs of Yi are the same as those of Tangwu,' indicating a shared cultural heritage.
  • 民穴居 - Means 'people live in caves,' describing a specific lifestyle.
  • 飞路之民 - Refers to a group of people who live in cold regions and live in caves, eating the roots of trees.
  • 丁○之民 - Refers to a group of people who live in cold regions, live in caves, and eat the meat of birds and mice. They are known as '名裘' (Mingqiu), possibly meaning 'famous fur.'
  • 朱梧县 - Refers to a specific place, possibly a county or region.
  • 其民服役 - Means 'the people serve as laborers,' indicating a form of labor or service.
  • 依海际居 - Means 'live near the coast,' describing a coastal lifestyle.
  • 产子，以沙石自拥 - Means 'when giving birth, they use sand and stones to support themselves,' describing a unique birthing practice.
  • 不食米 - Means 'do not eat rice,' indicating a different dietary habit.
  • 正资鱼以为生气 - Means 'rely solely on fish for sustenance,' emphasizing their dependence on fish.
  • 吴国西有具区泽 - Refers to a specific geographical feature, a lake or marsh to the west of Wu.
  • 包山 - Refers to a specific mountain, possibly a notable landmark.
  • 洞庭宝室 - Refers to a specific place, possibly a sacred or valuable site.
  • 入地下潜行，通琅琊东武 - Means 'enter underground and travel to Langya Dongwu,' describing a subterranean passage.
  • 寰宇记九十一 - Refers to a specific entry in a geographical or historical record.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 黄帝轩辕 - Refers to the legendary Yellow Emperor, a central figure in Chinese mythology and history.
  • 茄丰 - A name of a minister or official during the time of the Yellow Emperor.
  • 奇肱氏 - A mythical figure known for his skill in crafting flying vehicles, possibly a reference to a legendary inventor.
  • 君子之国 - A land of the virtuous or noble people, often used in classical texts to describe an idealized society.
  • 伊俗与唐吾同俗 - Indicates a shared cultural heritage between the Yi and Tangwu peoples, possibly reflecting historical or mythological connections.
  • 琅邪 - A region in ancient China, known for its historical significance.
  • 吴国 - Refers to the state of Wu, a powerful kingdom during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 具区泽 - A specific lake or marsh, possibly the Taihu Lake, known for its importance in Chinese geography.
  • 包山 - A specific mountain, possibly a notable landmark in the region.
  • 洞庭宝室 - A specific place, possibly a sacred or valuable site, often associated with the legend of the Eight Immortals.
  • 琅琊东武 - A specific region, possibly a city or county in the Langya area.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence describes a group of people who were punished and exiled, and then became known as '扶伏民.' The sentence structure is complex, with multiple clauses and a long distance mentioned.
  • This sentence describes a group of people who undergo a transformation process, eating leaves for several years and eventually growing wings. The sentence is elliptical and requires careful interpretation.
  • This sentence introduces a mythical figure known for his skill in crafting flying vehicles. The sentence is concise but rich in imagery.
  • This sentence describes an idealized land with a large territory and abundant hibiscus flowers. The sentence is simple but evocative.
  • This sentence describes a group of people who share a similar culture and live in caves, located a great distance from the Jade Gate.
  • This sentence describes a group of people who live in cold regions, in caves, and eat the roots of trees. The sentence is straightforward but provides a vivid picture.
  • This sentence describes a group of people who live in cold regions, in caves, and eat the meat of birds and mice. They are known as '名裘' (Mingqiu), possibly meaning 'famous fur.'
  • This sentence describes a specific place and its inhabitants, who serve as laborers, live near the coast, and have unique birthing and dietary practices.
  • This sentence describes a specific geographical feature, a lake with a notable mountain and a sacred site.
  • This sentence describes a subterranean passage connecting two regions.

Proper Nouns

  • 黄帝轩辕 - The Yellow Emperor, a central figure in Chinese mythology.
  • 茄丰 - A minister or official during the time of the Yellow Emperor.
  • 奇肱氏 - A mythical figure known for his skill in crafting flying vehicles.
  • 君子之国 - A land of the virtuous or noble people.
  • 伊俗 - The customs of the Yi people.
  • 唐吾 - Another group of people with similar customs.
  • 琅邪 - A region in ancient China.
  • 吴国 - The state of Wu.
  • 具区泽 - A specific lake or marsh.
  • 包山 - A specific mountain.
  • 洞庭宝室 - A specific place, possibly a sacred or valuable site.
  • 琅琊东武 - A specific region.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 浮天载地 - Poetic description of water's omnipresence and its role in sustaining life.
  • 高下无所不至 - Reaches every high and low place, moistening all things.
  • 水灌之而不已 - Water flows into it without stopping.
  • 鸿毛不能起也 - Even a goose feather cannot float on it.
  • 举一螯能加于山 - Lifting one claw can make it higher than a mountain.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 蜀郡有青城山 - Refers to Qingcheng Mountain in Shu Commandery, a famous Taoist mountain in Sichuan.
  • 彭城北有九里山 - Refers to Jiu Li Mountain north of Pengcheng, which is modern-day Xuzhou in Jiangsu Province.
  • 琅琊 - Refers to Langya, a region in Shandong.
  • 王屋 - Refers to Wangwu Mountain, a famous Taoist mountain in Henan.
  • 昆仑 - Refers to Kunlun Mountain, a mythical mountain in Chinese mythology.
  • 东海之沃焦 - Refers to Woji, a mythical mountain in the East Sea.
  • 昆仑之弱水 - Refers to the weak water of Kunlun, a mythical river in Chinese mythology.
  • 北海之蟹 - Refers to a mythical giant crab in the North Sea.

Proper Nouns

  • 蜀郡 - Shu Commandery, a historical administrative region in Sichuan.
  • 青城山 - Qingcheng Mountain, a famous Taoist mountain in Sichuan.
  • 彭城 - Pengcheng, an ancient name for Xuzhou in Jiangsu.
  • 九里山 - Jiu Li Mountain, a mountain north of Pengcheng.
  • 琅琊 - Langya, a region in Shandong.
  • 王屋 - Wangwu Mountain, a famous Taoist mountain in Henan.
  • 昆仑 - Kunlun Mountain, a mythical mountain in Chinese mythology.
  • 东海 - East Sea, a body of water in Chinese mythology.
  • 沃焦 - Woji, a mythical mountain in the East Sea.
  • 北海 - North Sea, a body of water in Chinese mythology.
  • 蟹 - Crab, a mythical giant crab in Chinese mythology.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 东南之大者，巨鳌焉 - Literal phrase meaning 'the greatest in the southeast is the giant turtle,' where '巨鳌' (giant turtle) is used to describe a mythical creature.
  • 以背负蓬莱山，周回千里 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'carrying the Mount Penglai on its back, with a circumference of a thousand miles.'
  • 东方之东海，有大鱼焉 - Literal phrase meaning 'in the eastern sea, there is a great fish.'
  • 广记引作东方之大者东海鱼焉 - Variant reading from the *Guangji* (Comprehensive Records) meaning 'in the eastern sea, there is a great fish.'
  • 行海者一日逢鱼头，七日逢鱼尾 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'those who travel the sea will encounter the fish's head for one day and the fish's tail for seven days.'
  • 其产则三百里为血 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'its spawning will turn three hundred miles into blood.'
  • 天下之高者，有扶桑无枝木焉 - Literal phrase meaning 'the highest in the world is the branchless tree of Fusang.'
  • 上至于天，盘蜿而下屈 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'reaching up to the heavens, coiling and bending downward.'
  • 木子之大者，有积石山之桃实焉 - Literal phrase meaning 'the largest of the fruit-bearing trees is the peach of Mount Jishan.'
  • 大如十斛笼 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'as large as a ten-ho container.'
  • 东方有柴都焉，在齐国 - Literal phrase meaning 'in the east is a place called Chaidu, in the state of Qi.'
  • 有山，山上有泉，如井状，深不测 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'there is a mountain, with a spring on top, like a well, with an unfathomable depth.'
  • 春夏常出雨雹 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'in spring and summer, it often produces rain and hail.'
  • 人以柴木塞之，则不出 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'people use firewood to block it, then it does not produce.'
  • 不柴塞，则出也 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'if not blocked with firewood, it will produce.'
  • 故曰柴都焉 - Concluding phrase meaning 'thus it is called Chaidu.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 巨鳌 - Refers to a mythical giant turtle, often associated with Mount Penglai, a legendary island in Chinese mythology.
  • 蓬莱山 - Refers to Mount Penglai, a mythical mountain in the East Sea, often associated with immortality and the abode of immortals.
  • 东海 - Refers to the East Sea, a mythical sea in Chinese mythology, often associated with the abode of immortals and mythical creatures.
  • 扶桑 - Refers to a mythical tree in Chinese mythology, often associated with the rising sun and the east.
  • 积石山 - Refers to Mount Jishan, a mythical mountain in Chinese mythology, often associated with the abode of immortals and mythical creatures.
  • 齐国 - Refers to the state of Qi, one of the major states during the Warring States period in Chinese history.
  • 柴都 - Refers to a place called Chaidu, mentioned in the text, which is associated with a spring that produces hail in spring and summer.

Proper Nouns

  • 巨鳌 - The giant turtle.
  • 蓬莱山 - Mount Penglai.
  • 东海 - The East Sea.
  • 扶桑 - The branchless tree of Fusang.
  • 积石山 - Mount Jishan.
  • 齐国 - The state of Qi.
  • 柴都 - The place Chaidu.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 南方有炎火山焉 - Classical locative phrase meaning 'there is a volcanic mountain in the south.'
  • 御览引有火字在扶南国之东 - Note from *Yuyan* (Imperial Inspection) indicating that the word 'fire' appears in the eastern part of Funan.
  • 加营国之北 - Indicates that it is also located north of Jia Ying.
  • 诸薄国之西 - Indicates that it is west of Zhubo.
  • 山从四月而火生 - Means 'the mountain starts to emit fire in April.'
  • 十二月火灭 - Means 'the fire extinguishes in December.'
  • 正月二月三月火不然 - Means 'in January, February, and March, the fire does not burn.'
  • 山上但出云气 - Means 'the mountain only emits clouds.'
  • 而草木生叶枝条 - Means 'and grass and trees grow leaves and branches.'
  • 至四月火然 - Means 'in April, the fire burns.'
  • 草木叶落 - Means 'grass and trees lose their leaves.'
  • 如中国寒时草木叶落也 - Means 'just like in China during cold weather, grass and trees lose their leaves.'
  • 行人以正月二月三月行过此山下 - Means 'travelers pass by the foot of this mountain in January, February, and March.'
  • 取柴以御览引作取此木为薪 - Means 'they take firewood or cut the wood for firewood.'
  • 然之无尽时 - Means 'it burns without end.'
  • 取其皮绩之 - Means 'they take its bark and weave it.'
  • 以为火浣布 - Means 'to make fire-washed cloth.'
  • 类聚八十 - Refers to the *Leiji* (Classified Records), volume 80.
  • 御览八百六十八 - Refers to the *Yuyan* (Imperial Inspection), volume 868.
  • 事类赋注八 - Refers to the *Shileifu Zushi* (Annotations on the Categories of Assignments), volume 8.
  • 北方有钟山焉 - Classical locative phrase meaning 'there is a Bell Mountain in the north.'
  • 左目为日 - Means 'the left eye is the sun.'
  • 右目为月 - Means 'the right eye is the moon.'
  • 开左目为昼 - Means 'opening the left eye is daytime.'
  • 开右目为夜 - Means 'opening the right eye is nighttime.'
  • 开口为春夏 - Means 'opening the mouth is spring and summer.'
  • 闭口为秋冬 - Means 'closing the mouth is autumn and winter.'
  • 东南有桃都山 - Classical locative phrase meaning 'there is a Peach Capital Mountain in the southeast.'
  • 上有大树 - Means 'there is a large tree on top.'
  • 名曰桃都 - Means 'named Peach Capital.'
  • 枝相去三千里 - Means 'the branches are three thousand li apart.'
  • 上有一天鸡 - Means 'there is a heavenly rooster on top.'
  • 日初出 - Means 'when the sun first rises.'
  • 光照此木 - Means 'its light illuminates this tree.'
  • 天鸡则鸣 - Means 'the heavenly rooster then crows.'
  • 群鸡皆随之鸣 - Means 'all other chickens follow and crow.'
  • 齐民要术六 - Refers to the *Qimin Yaoshu* (Essential Techniques for the Common People), volume 6.
  • 类聚九十一 - Refers to the *Leiji* (Classified Records), volume 91.
  • 御览九百十八下 - Refers to the *Yuyan* (Imperial Inspection), volume 918.
  • 有二神 - Means 'there are two deities.'
  • 左名隆 - Means 'the left one is named Long.'
  • 右名○ - Means 'the right one is named ○ (unclear).'
  • 玉烛宝典一 - Refers to the *Yuzhu Baojian* (Treasure Mirror of the Four Seasons), volume 1.
  • 注并执苇索伺不祥之鬼 - Means 'they hold reed ropes to watch for evil spirits.'
  • 得而煞之 - Means 'and kill them.'
  • 已上三句以玉烛宝典引括地图补 - Means 'the above three sentences are added based on the *Yuzhu Baojian* and the map.'
  • 今人正朝作两桃人立门旁 - Means 'today people make two peach figures stand at the door.'
  • 以雄鸡毛置索中 - Means 'they place rooster feathers in the rope.'
  • 盖遗象也 - Means 'this is a remnant of the past.'
  • 御览二十九 - Refers to the *Yuyan* (Imperial Inspection), volume 29.
  • 玉烛宝典一 - Refers to the *Yuzhu Baojian* (Treasure Mirror of the Four Seasons), volume 1.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 炎火山 - A volcanic mountain known for its fiery eruptions.
  • 扶南国 - An ancient kingdom in Southeast Asia.
  • 加营国 - An ancient kingdom in the region.
  • 诸薄国 - An ancient kingdom in the region.
  • 中国 - Refers to China, emphasizing the comparison with Chinese weather patterns.
  • 火浣布 - A type of cloth made from the bark of a tree, which can be washed with fire.
  • 钟山 - A mythical mountain in the north.
  • 桃都山 - A mythical mountain in the southeast.
  • 天鸡 - A mythical rooster that crows at dawn.
  • 玉烛宝典 - An ancient text on the four seasons and their corresponding rituals.
  • 齐民要术 - An ancient agricultural text.
  • 类聚 - An ancient encyclopedic text.
  • 御览 - An ancient encyclopedic text.
  • 事类赋注 - Annotations on the *Shileifu* (Categories of Assignments).
  • 正朝 - The first day of the lunar year, a significant day in traditional Chinese culture.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '南方有炎火山焉' sets the location and introduces the main subject.
  • The clause '御览引有火字在扶南国之东，加营国之北，诸薄国之西' provides additional geographical context.
  • The sequence '山从四月而火生；十二月火灭；正月二月三月火不然' describes the cyclical nature of the volcanic activity.
  • The clause '山上但出云气，而草木生叶枝条' contrasts the volcanic activity with the natural growth of plants.
  • The clause '至四月火然，草木叶落' continues the cyclical description.
  • The clause '如中国寒时草木叶落也' provides a comparison to familiar conditions.
  • The clause '行人以正月二月三月行过此山下' introduces the travelers and their activities.
  • The clause '取柴以御览引作取此木为薪' explains the use of the wood.
  • The clause '然之无尽时' emphasizes the continuous burning.
  • The clause '取其皮绩之，以为火浣布' describes the making of the cloth.
  • The references '类聚八十，御览八百六十八，事类赋注八' indicate the sources of the information.
  • The phrase '北方有钟山焉' sets the location and introduces the main subject.
  • The clause '山上石首如人首' describes the unique feature of the mountain.
  • The clause '左目为日，右目为月' describes the symbolic features.
  • The clause '开左目为昼，开右目为夜' explains the symbolic functions.
  • The clause '开口为春夏，闭口为秋冬' explains the symbolic functions.
  • The phrase '东南有桃都山' sets the location and introduces the main subject.
  • The clause '上有大树，名曰桃都' describes the tree.
  • The clause '枝相去三千里' emphasizes the vastness of the tree.
  • The clause '上有一天鸡' introduces the mythical rooster.
  • The sequence '日初出，光照此木，天鸡则鸣，群鸡皆随之鸣' describes the daily cycle.
  • The references '齐民要术六，类聚九十一，御览九百十八下' indicate the sources of the information.
  • The clause '有二神，左名隆，右名○' introduces the deities.
  • The reference '玉烛宝典一，注并执苇索伺不祥之鬼，得而煞之' indicates the sources of the information.
  • The clause '已上三句以玉烛宝典引括地图补' explains the addition of the sentences.
  • The clause '今人正朝作两桃人立门旁' describes the practice of making peach figures at the door.
  • The clause '以雄鸡毛置索中' explains the use of rooster feathers in the rope.
  • The clause '盖遗象也' explains the cultural significance of the practice.
  • The reference '御览二十九，玉烛宝典一' indicates the sources of the information.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 大月氏及西胡 - Refers to the Great Yuezhi and the Western Hu, nomadic peoples in Central Asia.
  • 割取其肉三四斤 - Literally 'cut and take its flesh, three or four pounds,' describing a ritual or practice involving the ox.
  • 其肉已復 - Means 'the flesh has already returned,' likely referring to a miraculous or supernatural event.
  • 创即愈也 - Means 'the wound heals immediately,' indicating a healing or restorative effect.
  • 漢人入此國，見牛不知以為珍異 - Means 'when Han people entered this country, they did not know that the ox was considered a treasure,' suggesting cultural unfamiliarity.
  • 吾國有蟲，大小如指，名為蠶 - Means 'we have a creature, as big as a finger, called a silkworm,' referring to the silkworm's role in silk production.
  • 食桑葉，為人吐絲 - Means 'it eats mulberry leaves and spits out silk for humans,' describing the silkworm's life cycle.
  • 復作及 - Likely a variant or error in the text, possibly referring to the ox's miraculous healing or transformation.
  • 玄菟北有山，山有花，人取紡織為布 - Means 'north of Xuantu, there is a mountain with flowers, and people pick them to spin and weave into cloth.'
  • 东海之东，有树名为白蒙，其汁可为脂，色白如脂，味甘 - Means 'east of the Eastern Sea, there is a tree named Bai Meng, whose juice can be made into fat, white like fat, and sweet in taste.'
  • 荆州有树名乌臼，实如胡麻子，其汁如脂，其味亦如猪脂味也 - Means 'in Jingzhou, there is a tree named Wujiao, whose fruit is like sesame seeds, and its juice is like fat, with a taste similar to pork fat.'

  • 玄菟 - Refers to Xuantu, an ancient commandery in the northern part of the Korean Peninsula or Manchuria.
  • 荆州 - Refers to Jingzhou, one of the nine administrative divisions of China during the Han dynasty, located in the central-southern region.
  • 东海 - Refers to the Eastern Sea, likely the East China Sea or the Yellow Sea, depending on the context.
  • 白蒙 - A tree mentioned in the text, likely a local or regional plant with medicinal or practical uses.
  • 乌臼 - A tree mentioned in the text, likely a local or regional plant with medicinal or practical uses.

  • 此青牛是万年木精也 - Means 'this green ox is the spirit of a ten-thousand-year-old tree.'

  • 鳥無子，喜取人子養之，以為子 - Describes the creature's behavior of adopting human children.

  • 愛以血點其衣為志 - Describes the creature's habit of marking clothes with blood to identify them.
  • 即取小儿也 - Indicates that the creature takes the child after marking the clothes.

  • 得而服之，使人神仙 - Literal expression meaning 'if one obtains and consumes it, it can make one a deity.'
  • 千歲伏翼，色白 - Literal expression meaning 'a thousand-year-old bat, its color is white.'
  • 得食之，壽萬歲 - Literal expression meaning 'if one eats it, one will live ten thousand years.'
  • 千歲之鶴，隨時鳴 - Literal expression meaning 'a thousand-year-old crane, it calls out according to the time.'
  • 千歲之燕，戶北向 - Literal expression meaning 'a thousand-year-old swallow, it faces north when perched.'
  • 千歲之龜，能與人語 - Literal expression meaning 'a thousand-year-old turtle, it can speak with humans.'
  • 千歲蟾蜍，頭生角 - Literal expression meaning 'a thousand-year-old toad, it has horns on its head.'
  • 得而食之，壽千歲 - Literal expression meaning 'if one obtains and eats it, one will live a thousand years.'
  • 又能食山精 - Literal expression meaning 'it can also consume mountain spirits.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 狐 - Refers to a fox, often associated with cunning and transformation in Chinese folklore.
  • 鼠 - Refers to a mouse, often associated with transformation and deification in Chinese folklore.
  • 伏翼 - Refers to a bat, often associated with longevity and transformation in Chinese folklore.
  • 鶴 - Refers to a crane, often associated with longevity and wisdom in Chinese folklore.
  • 燕 - Refers to a swallow, often associated with longevity and auspiciousness in Chinese folklore.
  • 龜 - Refers to a turtle, often associated with longevity and wisdom in Chinese folklore.
  • 蟾蜍 - Refers to a toad, often associated with transformation and longevity in Chinese folklore.
  • 山精 - Refers to mountain spirits, often associated with mystical and supernatural qualities in Chinese folklore.

Proper Nouns

  • 初學記 - The *Initial Studies Record*, a classical Chinese encyclopedia.
  • 御覽 - The *Imperial Reader*, a classical Chinese encyclopedia.
  • 廣記 - The *Comprehensive Records*, a classical Chinese encyclopedia.
  • 類聚 - The *Classified Collections*, a classical Chinese encyclopedia.
  • 酉陽雜俎 - The *Miscellaneous Records of the West and East*, a classical Chinese encyclopedia.
  • 春秋左傳文四年正義 - A commentary on the *Spring and Autumn Annals* (春秋左传) for the fourth year of Duke Wen (文四年).
  • 玉燭寶典 - The *Precious Canon of the Jade Candle*, a classical Chinese encyclopedia.
  • 廣韻 - The *Comprehensive Rhyme*, a classical Chinese rhyme dictionary.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 山精如人 - Literal description of a mountain spirit resembling a human.
  • 一足长三四尺 - Describes the mountain spirit as having one foot that is three to four feet long.
  • 食山蟹 - Indicates that the mountain spirit eats mountain crabs.
  • 夜出昼藏 - Describes the mountain spirit as appearing at night and hiding during the day.
  • 人不能见 - Means that humans cannot see the mountain spirit.
  • 夜闻其声 - Indicates that humans can hear the mountain spirit at night.
  • 千岁蟾蜍食之 - Means that a thousand-year-old toad eats the mountain spirit.
  • 蜮长三四寸 - Describes the shape and size of the 'yù' (蜮), a type of creature.
  • 蟾蜍，鸑鷟鸳鸯悉食之 - Indicates that toads, phoenixes, and mandarin ducks all eat the 'yù'.
  • 广韵二十五德 - Refers to a specific section in the 'Guangyun' (广韵), a rhyme dictionary.
  • 水狐者，视其形虫也 - Describes the 'shuǐ hú' (水狐) as a creature that looks like an insect.
  • 其气乃鬼也 - Indicates that the 'shuǐ hú' has a ghostly nature.
  • 长三四寸 - Describes the length of the 'shuǐ hú' as three to four inches.
  • 其色黑 - Describes the color of the 'shuǐ hú' as black.
  • 广寸许 - Indicates that the 'shuǐ hú' is about an inch wide.
  • 背上有甲，厚三分许 - Describes the 'shuǐ hú' as having a shell on its back that is about three inches thick.
  • 其头有物，向前如角状 - Describes the 'shuǐ hú' as having something on its head that looks like a horn.
  • 见人则气射人 - Indicates that when the 'shuǐ hú' sees a person, it shoots a gas at them.
  • 去二三步即射人 - Means that the 'shuǐ hú' can shoot a gas at a person from two to three steps away.
  • 中，十人六七人死 - Indicates that out of ten people, six to seven die when hit by the gas.
  • 御览九百五十 - Refers to a specific section in the 'Taiping Guangji' (太平广记).
  • 广记四百七十三题感应经引玄中记云 - Refers to a specific section in the 'Guangji' (广记) that cites the 'Xuanzhong Ji' (玄中记).
  • 经史证类本草二十二引云 - Refers to a specific section in the 'Jing Shi Zheng Lei Ben Cao' (经史证类本草) that cites a source.
  • 江淮间谓之短狐射工通为溪病 - Indicates that in the Jianghuai region, the 'shuǐ hú' is called 'duan hu' (短狐) and is known as 'she gong' (射工) and is associated with 'xi bing' (溪病).
  • 此既其虫故能相压伏也 - Means that because the 'shuǐ hú' is a creature, it can suppress other creatures.
  • 越燕，斑胸 - Describes the 'yue yan' (越燕) as having a speckled chest.
  • 声小 - Indicates that the 'yue yan' has a small voice.
  • 胡燕，红襟 - Describes the 'hu yan' (胡燕) as having a red collar.
  • 声大 - Indicates that the 'hu yan' has a loud voice.
  • 玉精为白虎 - Means that the essence of jade becomes a white tiger.
  • 金精为车马 - Means that the essence of gold becomes a carriage and horses.
  • 铜精为僮奴 - Means that the essence of copper becomes servants.
  • 御览八百十三引作奴婢 - Refers to a specific section in the 'Taiping Guangji' (太平广记) that cites a source.
  • 铅精为老妇 - Means that the essence of lead becomes an old woman.
  • 御览八百八十六 - Refers to a specific section in the 'Taiping Guangji' (太平广记).
  • 广韵二十三锡引云 - Refers to a specific section in the 'Guangyun' (广韵) that cites a source.
  • 铅锡之精为婢 - Means that the essence of lead and tin becomes a maid.
  • 御览八百十二引作为老婢 - Refers to a specific section in the 'Taiping Guangji' (太平广记) that cites a source.
  • 又九百十二引作为狐狸 - Refers to another specific section in the 'Taiping Guangji' (太平广记) that cites a source.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 松脂沦入地中，千岁为茯苓，伏神 - Describes the transformation of pine resin into *fu ling* (poria cocos) and *fu shen* (a type of fungus) after a thousand years.
  • 枫脂沦入地中，千秋为虎珀 - Describes the transformation of maple resin into *hu bo* (amber) after a thousand years.
  • 削玉如铁刀削木 - Describes the sharpness of a diamond knife, capable of cutting jade like an iron knife cuts wood.
  • 大者长尺许，小者如稻米 - Describes the size range of diamonds, from large to small.
  • 欲刻玉时，当作大金镮，着手指间，开其背如月，以割玉刀内镮中，以刻玉 - Describes the process of using a diamond knife to carve jade, involving a large gold ring.
  • 五肉七菜，胜腌腥臊 - Describes a dietary preference for five meats and seven vegetables over preserved and strong-smelling foods.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 大秦国 - Refers to the Roman Empire, often used in ancient Chinese texts to describe the Western world.
  • 西海 - Refers to the western sea, likely the Mediterranean Sea.
  • 天竺 - Refers to India, a common term in ancient Chinese texts.
  • 大月氐 - Refers to a historical region, possibly the Kushan Empire.
  • 五色颇黎 - Refers to colored glass, often used in jewelry and decorative items.
  • 木难 - Refers to a type of gemstone, possibly a precious stone or mineral.
